seoTitle: How to Do Progress Billing in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to set up progress billing in QuickBooks Online so long-term projects like construction jobs can be invoiced in stages instead of all at once.
intro: For projects that span weeks or months, billing everything at the end isn't practical, which is where progress billing comes in. This guide walks through how to invoice a project in stages using QuickBooks Online's progress billing feature.

steps.heading: Create an estimate for the full project
steps.body: Start with an estimate that covers the total scope and value of the project.

steps.heading: Turn on progress invoicing
steps.body: Enable the progress invoicing setting so you can bill a percentage or portion of the estimate at a time.

steps.heading: Create a progress invoice from the estimate
steps.body: Generate an invoice from the estimate and specify how much of the total to bill for this stage.

steps.heading: Repeat for each project milestone
steps.body: Continue creating progress invoices from the same estimate as work is completed until the full amount is billed.

faq.question: Do I need to create a new estimate for each billing stage?
faq.answer: No, you create one estimate for the full project and generate multiple invoices from it as work progresses.

faq.question: Can I bill different percentages at each stage?
faq.answer: Yes, progress invoicing lets you specify a different percentage or amount each time you bill.

faq.question: What happens once the full estimate has been billed?
faq.answer: Once the total estimate amount has been invoiced across all stages, no further progress invoices can be created from it.

faq.question: Is progress billing available on every QuickBooks Online plan?
faq.answer: Progress invoicing is typically available on higher-tier plans, so confirm your subscription supports it.
